body: private static int hexToBinary(final byte b) throws IOException {
throw new IOException("Invalid quoted printable encoding: not a valid hex digit: " + b);

IOException.reasoning: In Python, `Exception` is the base class for all exceptions. It's used to catch any type of exception that might occur during execution. In this case, it would be used to catch any type of I/O related errors.

translation: if i < 48 or (57 < i and i < 65) or (70 < i and i < 97) or i > 102:
raise IOError("Invalid quoted printable encoding: not a valid hex digit: " + str(b))
